steps.text: Subject extramural research applications to external peer review by a Federal Advisory Committee.
Submit written justification requesting an exception from the peer review policy.
Grant approval for the exception by the CDC Office of Science (OS) or their designee in consultation with the Office of Financial Resources (OFR).
Conduct first-level review by a panel of experts to evaluate the scientific and technical merit of research applications.

Conduct second-level review by a separate senior advisory panel to evaluate the preliminary recommendations from the first-level review in the context of program relevance or priorities, policy considerations, and fiscal capacity.
Conduct peer review of intramural research and scientific programs.
Conduct technical evaluation review of contract proposals (Step 1).
Conduct review to determine competitive range and negotiate best and final offers (Step 2).
Review and select Task Orders (TOs) using existing OFR guidance and procedures.
Consult with OFR for specific guidance related to Peer Review of Research Contracts.
Implement this policy and annually report planned and completed peer review activities to the OS.
Ensure reviews are conducted by experts external to CDC, not affiliated with the program, and without conflict of interest.
Provide overall guidance to CIOs to implement and assess the utility and effectiveness of the peer review process.
Provide written assurance that reviews are free of real or perceived conflicts of interest.

Base decisions regarding whether a project is research or non-research on guidance in the CDC Human Subjects Research document 'Policy for Distinguishing Public Health Research and Public Health Nonresearch'.

preconditions.text: A justified emergency situation exists preventing standard external peer review.

If reviewers are recruited on an ad hoc basis, outside of a Federal Advisory Committee Act (FACA) committee, they must provide individual and independent comments of findings, and consensus decisions must be avoided.
In consultation with the CIO director or his/her designee.
In coordination with CDC’s Federal Advisory Committee Management Officer.
In consultation with the OFR, when appropriate.

constraints.text: All research and scientific programs conducted or funded by CDC are subject to periodic external peer review.
This policy applies to extramural research funded by grants or cooperative agreements, including institutional awards to research centers.

Scientific programs (including research and non-research, and intramural and extramural activities) conducted or funded by CDC are subject to external peer review for scientific and technical quality.
Core service activities, such as animal laboratory facilities or clinical pathology laboratories, may be subject to accreditation or audit review and also require peer review.
Peer review of intramural research and scientific programs must address program quality, approach, direction, capability, and integrity.
The contract administration process, including selection criteria and review, is regulated by the FAR (Federal Acquisition Regulations).
The contract award must be made by the OFR contracting officer in consultation with the CIO director or his/her designee.
Reviews must be conducted by experts external to CDC, not affiliated with the program, and without conflict of interest.
